continuationToken.description: The continuation token to be used to fetch the next page of items

hasNextPage.description: Whether there are more items available that can be fetched by supplying the continuation token

orderBy.description: The current sorting order of the items

title.default: One or more validation errors occurred.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the party, if a person.

hasKeyRole.description:
Whether the authenticated user has a key role in the party.
            
Read more about key roles (norwegian) at https://docs.altinn.studio/nb/altinn-studio/reference/configuration/authorization/guidelines_authorization/roles_and_rights/roles_er/#nøkkelroller

hasOnlyAccessToSubParties.description: If the authenticated user has only access to sub parties of this party, and not this party itself.

isAccessManager.description:
Whether the authenticated user is an access manager of the party.
            
Read more about access managers (norwegian) at https://docs.altinn.studio/nb/altinn-studio/reference/configuration/authorization/guidelines_authorization/roles_and_rights/roles_altinn/altinn_roles_administration/#tilgangsstrying

isCurrentEndUser.description: Whether this party represents the authenticated user.

isDeleted.description: Whether the party is deleted or not

isMainAdministrator.description:
Whether the authenticated user is the main administrator of the party
            
Read more about main administrator (norwegian) at https://docs.altinn.studio/nb/altinn-studio/reference/configuration/authorization/guidelines_authorization/roles_and_rights/roles_altinn/altinn_roles_administration/#hovedadministrator

name.description: The name of the party (verbatim from CCR, usually in all caps)

party.description: The party identifier in URN format

partyId.description: The numeric identifier for the party.

partyType.description: The type of the party, either "Organization" or "Person".

subParties.description: The sub parties of this party, if any. The sub party uses the same data model.

isAuthorized.description:
True if the authenticated user is authorized for this content. If not, the endpoints will
be replaced with a fixed placeholder. Can be null if not applicable.

mediaType.description: Media type of the content, this can also indicate that the content is embeddable.

value.description: A list of localizations for the content.

languageCode.description: The language code of the localization in ISO 639-1 format.

value.description: The localized text (or URL if a front-channel embed).

actorId.description: The identifier (national identity number or organization number) of the actor.

actorType.description: The type of actor; either the service owner, or someone representing the party.

additionalInfo.description: Additional information about the dialog, this may contain Markdown.

extendedStatus.description: Used as the human-readable label used to describe the "ExtendedStatus" field.

mainContentReference.description:
Front-channel embedded content. Used to dynamically embed content in the frontend from an external URL.
Content value will be masked if the user is not authorized to read main content.

senderName.description: Overridden sender name. If not supplied, assume "org" as the sender name.

summary.description: A short summary of the dialog and its current state.

activities.description: An immutable list of activities associated with the dialog.

apiActions.description: The API actions associated with the dialog. Should be used in specialized, non-browser-based integrations.

attachments.description: The attachments associated with the dialog (on an aggregate level).

contentUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the dialog content was last updated.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the dialog was created.

dialogToken.description:
The dialog token. May be used (if supported) against external URLs referred to in this dialog's apiActions,
transmissions or attachments. It should also be used for front-channel embeds.

dueAt.description: The due date for the dialog. Dialogs past due date might be marked as such in frontends but will still be available.

endUserContext.description: Metadata about the dialog owned by end-users.

expiresAt.description:
The expiration date for the dialog. This is the last date when the dialog is available for the end user.
            
After this date is passed, the dialog will be considered expired and no longer available for the end user in any
API. If not supplied, the dialog will be considered to never expire. This field can be changed by the service
owner after the dialog has been created.

extendedStatus.description:
Arbitrary string with a service-specific indicator of status, typically used to indicate a fine-grained state of
the dialog to further specify the "status" enum.
            
Refer to the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details on the possible values (if
in use).

externalReference.description:
Arbitrary string with a service-specific reference to an external system or service.
            
Refer to the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

fromPartyTransmissionsCount.description: The number of transmissions sent by a party representative

fromServiceOwnerTransmissionsCount.description: The number of transmissions sent by a service owner

guiActions.description: The GUI actions associated with the dialog. Should be used in browser-based interactive frontends.

hasUnopenedContent.description:
Indicates whether the dialog contains content that has not been viewed or opened by the user yet.
            
Obsolete: A dialog is now considered 'seen' when the dialog has:
- At least one entry in SeenSinceLastContentUpdate and
- No system label MarkedAsUnopened.

id.description: The unique identifier for the dialog in UUIDv7 format.

isApiOnly.description: Indicates if this dialog is intended for API consumption only and should not be shown in frontends aimed at humans.

org.description: The service owner code representing the organization (service owner) related to this dialog.

party.description: The party code representing the organization or person that the dialog belongs to in URN format.

precedingProcess.description: Optional preceding process identifier to indicate the business process that preceded the process indicated in the "Process" field. Cannot be set without also "Process" being set.

process.description: Optional process identifier used to indicate a business process this dialog belongs to.

progress.description:
Advisory indicator of progress, represented as 1-100 percentage value. 100% representing a dialog that has come
to a natural completion (successful or not).

revision.description: The unique identifier for the revision in UUIDv4 format.

seenSinceLastContentUpdate.description: The list of seen log entries for the dialog newer than the dialog ContentUpdatedAt date.

seenSinceLastUpdate.description: The list of seen log entries for the dialog newer than the dialog UpdatedAt date.

serviceResource.description:
The service identifier for the service that the dialog is related to in URN-format.
This corresponds to a service resource in the Altinn Resource Registry.

serviceResourceType.description: The ServiceResource type, as defined in Altinn Resource Registry (see ResourceType).

status.description: The aggregated status of the dialog.

systemLabel.description:
System defined label used to categorize dialogs.
This is obsolete and will only show; Default, Bin or Archive.
Use SystemLabels on EndUserContext instead.

transmissions.description: The immutable list of transmissions associated with the dialog.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the dialog was last updated.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the activity was created.

description.description: Unstructured text describing the activity. Only set if the activity type is "Information".

extendedType.description:
An arbitrary URI/URN with a service-specific activity type.
            
Consult the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

id.description: The unique identifier for the activity in UUIDv7 format.

performedBy.description: The actor that performed the activity.

transmissionId.description: If the activity is related to a particular transmission, this field will contain the transmission identifier.

action.description:
String identifier for the action, corresponding to the "action" attributeId used in the XACML service policy,
which by default is the policy belonging to the service referred to by "serviceResource" in the dialog.

authorizationAttribute.description:
Contains an authorization resource attributeId, that can used in custom authorization rules in the XACML service
policy, which by default is the policy belonging to the service referred to by "serviceResource" in the dialog.
            
Can also be used to refer to other service policies.

endpoints.description: The endpoints associated with the action.

id.description: The unique identifier for the action in UUIDv7 format.

isAuthorized.description:
True if the authenticated user is authorized for this action. If not, the action will not be available
and all endpoints will be replaced with a fixed placeholder.

name.description: The logical name of the operation the API action refers to.

deprecated.description: Boolean indicating if the endpoint is deprecated. Integrators should migrate to endpoints with a higher version.

documentationUrl.description:
Link to service provider documentation for the endpoint. Used for service owners to provide documentation for
integrators. Should be a URL to a human-readable page.

httpMethod.description: The HTTP method that the endpoint expects for this action.

id.description: The unique identifier for the endpoint in UUIDv7 format.

requestSchema.description:
Link to the request schema for the endpoint. Used by service owners to provide documentation for integrators.
Dialogporten will not validate information on this endpoint.

responseSchema.description:
Link to the response schema for the endpoint. Used for service owners to provide documentation for integrators.
Dialogporten will not validate information on this endpoint.

sunsetAt.description:
Date and time when the service owner has indicated that endpoint will no longer function. Only set if the endpoint
is deprecated. Dialogporten will not enforce this date.

url.description:
The fully qualified URL of the API endpoint. Will be set to "urn:dialogporten:unauthorized" if the user is
not authorized to perform the action.

version.description:
Arbitrary string indicating the version of the endpoint.
            
Consult the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

displayName.description: The display name of the attachment that should be used in GUIs.

expiresAt.description: The UTC timestamp when the attachment expires and is no longer available.

id.description: The unique identifier for the attachment in UUIDv7 format.

name.description: The logical name of the attachment.

urls.description: The URLs associated with the attachment, each referring to a different representation of the attachment.

consumerType.description: What type of consumer the URL is intended for.

id.description: The unique identifier for the attachment URL in UUIDv7 format.

mediaType.description: The media type of the attachment.

url.description: The fully qualified URL of the attachment.

revision.description: The unique identifier for the end user context revision in UUIDv4 format.

systemLabels.description: System defined labels used to categorize dialogs.

action.description: The action identifier for the action, corresponding to the "action" attributeId used in the XACML service policy.

authorizationAttribute.description:
Contains an authorization resource attributeId, that can used in custom authorization rules in the XACML service
policy, which by default is the policy belonging to the service referred to by "serviceResource" in the dialog.
            
Can also be used to refer to other service policies.

httpMethod.description: The HTTP method that the frontend should use when redirecting the user.

id.description: The unique identifier for the action in UUIDv7 format.

isAuthorized.description: Whether the user is authorized to perform the action.

isDeleteDialogAction.description:
Indicates whether the action results in the dialog being deleted. Used by frontends to implement custom UX
for delete actions.

priority.description:
Indicates a priority for the action, making it possible for frontends to adapt GUI elements based on action
priority.

prompt.description:
If there should be a prompt asking the user for confirmation before the action is executed,
this field should contain the prompt text.

title.description: The title of the action, this should be short and in verb form.

url.description:
The fully qualified URL of the action, to which the user will be redirected when the action is triggered. Will be set to
"urn:dialogporten:unauthorized" if the user is not authorized to perform the action.

id.description: The unique identifier for the seen log entry in UUIDv7 format.

isCurrentEndUser.description: Flag indicating whether the seen log entry was created by the current end user.

isViaServiceOwner.description:
Flag indicating whether the seen log entry was created via the service owner.
            
This is used when the service owner uses the service owner API to implement its own frontend.

seenAt.description: The timestamp when the dialog revision was seen.

seenBy.description: The actor that saw the dialog revision.

authorizationAttribute.description:
Contains an authorization resource attributeId, that can used in custom authorization rules in the XACML service
policy, which by default is the policy belonging to the service referred to by "serviceResource" in the dialog.
            
Can also be used to refer to other service policies.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the transmission was created.

extendedType.description:
Arbitrary URI/URN describing a service-specific transmission type.
            
Refer to the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

externalReference.description: Arbitrary string with a service-specific reference to an external system or service.

id.description: The unique identifier for the transmission in UUIDv7 format.

isAuthorized.description:
Flag indicating if the authenticated user is authorized for this transmission. If not, embedded content and
the attachments will not be available.

isOpened.description: Indicates whether the dialog transmission has been opened.

relatedTransmissionId.description: Reference to any other transmission that this transmission is related to.

sender.description: The actor that sent the transmission.

displayName.description: The display name of the attachment that should be used in GUIs.

expiresAt.description: The UTC timestamp when the attachment expires and is no longer available.

id.description: The unique identifier for the attachment in UUIDv7 format.

name.description: The logical name of the attachment.

urls.description: The URLs associated with the attachment, each referring to a different representation of the attachment.

consumerType.description: The type of consumer the URL is intended for.

id.description: The unique identifier for the attachment URL in UUIDv7 format.

mediaType.description: The media type of the attachment.

url.description:
The fully qualified URL of the attachment. Will be set to "urn:dialogporten:unauthorized" if the user is
not authorized to access the transmission.

contentReference.description: Front-channel embedded content. Used to dynamically embed content in the frontend from an external URL.

expiresAt.description: The UTC timestamp when the navigational action expires and is no longer available.

title.description: The title of the navigational action.

url.description:
The fully qualified URL of the navigational action. Will be set to \"urn:dialogporten:unauthorized\" if the user is
not authorized to access the transmission, or \"urn:dialogporten:expired\" if the action has expired.

displayName.description: The display name of the attachment that should be used in GUIs.

expiresAt.description: The UTC timestamp when the attachment expires and is no longer available.

name.description: The logical name of the attachment.

urls.description: The URLs associated with the attachment, each referring to a different representation of the attachment.

not authorized to access the transmission.

The fully qualified URL of the navigational action. Will be set to \"urn:dialogporten:unauthorized\" if the user is
not authorized to access the transmission, or \"urn:dialogporten:expired\" if the action has expired.

attachments.description: The attachments associated with the transmission.

authorizationAttribute.description: The authorization attribute associated with the transmission.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the transmission was created.

deletedAt.description: The date and time when the transmission was deleted, if applicable.

extendedType.description: The extended type URI for the transmission.

externalReference.description: Arbitrary string with a service-specific reference to an external system or service.

Flag indicating if the authenticated user is authorized for this transmission. If not, embedded content and
the attachments will not be available.

navigationalActions.description: The navigational actions associated with the transmission.

relatedTransmissionId.description: The unique identifier for the related transmission, if any.

sender.description: The sender actor information for the transmission.

extendedStatus.description: Used as the human-readable label used to describe the "ExtendedStatus" field.

senderName.description: Overridden sender name. If not supplied, assume "org" as the sender name.

summary.description: A short summary of the dialog and its current state.

content.description: The content of the dialog in search results.

contentUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the dialog content was last updated.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the dialog was created.

dueAt.description: The due date for the dialog. This is the last date when the dialog is expected to be completed.

endUserContext.description: Metadata about the dialog owned by end-users.

extendedStatus.description:
Arbitrary string with a service-specific indicator of status, typically used to indicate a fine-grained state of
the dialog to further specify the "status" enum.
            
Refer to the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details on the possible values (if
in use).

externalReference.description:
Arbitrary string with a service-specific reference to an external system or service.
            
Refer to the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

fromPartyTransmissionsCount.description: The number of transmissions sent by a party representative

fromServiceOwnerTransmissionsCount.description: The number of transmissions sent by the service owner

guiAttachmentCount.description: The number of attachments in the dialog made available for browser-based frontends.

hasUnopenedContent.description:
Indicates whether the dialog contains content that has not been viewed or opened by the user yet.
            
Obsolete: A dialog is now considered 'seen' when the dialog has:
- At least one entry in SeenSinceLastContentUpdate and
- No system label MarkedAsUnopened.

id.description: The unique identifier for the dialog in UUIDv7 format.

isApiOnly.description:
Indicates if this dialog is intended for API consumption only and should not be shown in frontends aimed at humans.
When true, human-readable content like title and summary are not required.

latestActivity.description: The latest entry in the dialog's activity log.

org.description: The service owner code representing the organization (service owner) related to this dialog.

party.description: The party code representing the organization or person that the dialog belongs to in URN format.

precedingProcess.description: Optional preceding process identifier to indicate the business process that preceded the process indicated in the "Process" field. Cannot be set without also "Process" being set.

process.description: Optional process identifier used to indicate a business process this dialog belongs to.

progress.description:
Advisory indicator of progress, represented as 1-100 percentage value. 100% representing a dialog that has come
to a natural completion (successful or not).

seenSinceLastContentUpdate.description: The list of seen log entries for the dialog newer than the dialog ContentUpdatedAt date.

seenSinceLastUpdate.description: The list of seen log entries for the dialog newer than the dialog UpdatedAt date.

serviceResource.description:
The service identifier for the service that the dialog is related to in URN-format.
This corresponds to a service resource in the Altinn Resource Registry.

serviceResourceType.description: The ServiceResource type, as defined in Altinn Resource Registry (see ResourceType).

status.description: The aggregated status of the dialog.

systemLabel.description:
System defined label used to categorize dialogs.
This is obsolete and will only show; Default, Bin or Archive.
Use SystemLabels on EndUserContext instead.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the dialog was last updated.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the activity was created.

description.description: Unstructured text describing the activity. Only set if the activity type is "Information".

An arbitrary string with a service-specific activity type.
            
Consult the service-specific documentation provided by the service owner for details (if in use).

performedBy.description: The actor that performed the activity.

transmissionId.description: If the activity is related to a particular transmission, this field will contain the transmission identifier.

Flag indicating whether the seen log entry was created via the service owner.
            
This is used when the service owner uses the service owner API to implement its own frontend.

not authorized to access the transmission.

The fully qualified URL of the navigational action. Will be set to \"urn:dialogporten:unauthorized\" if the user is

authorizationAttribute.description: The authorization attribute associated with the transmission.

deletedAt.description: The date and time when the transmission was deleted, if applicable.

extendedType.description: The extended type URI for the transmission.

Flag indicating if the authenticated user is authorized for this transmission. If not, embedded content and

addLabels.description: List of system labels to add to the target dialogs. If multiple instances of 'bin', 'archive', or 'default' are provided, the last one will be used.

dialogs.description: List of target dialog ids with optional revision ids

removeLabels.description: List of system labels to remove from the target dialogs. If 'bin' or 'archive' is removed, the 'default' label will be added automatically unless 'bin' or 'archive' is also in the AddLabels list.

systemLabels.description: List of system labels to set on target dialogs
systemLabels.x-deprecatedMessage: Use AddLabels instead. This property will be removed in a future version.

dialogId.description: Target dialog id for system labels

endUserContextRevision.description: Optional end user context revision to match against. If supplied and not matching current revision, the entire operation will fail.

addLabels.description: List of system labels to add to the target dialog. If multiple instances of 'bin', 'archive', or 'default' are provided, the last one will be used.

removeLabels.description: List of system labels to remove from the target dialog. If 'bin' or 'archive' is removed, the 'default' label will be added automatically unless 'bin' or 'archive' is also in the AddLabels list.

systemLabels.description: List of system labels to set on target dialogs
systemLabels.x-deprecatedMessage: Use AddLabels instead. This property will be removed in a future version.

JWTBearerAuth.description: Enter a JWT token to authorize the requests...

info.description:
Dialogporten API description for both enduser and serviceowner users, as well as open metadata information for public key material.

All operations* described within this document require authentication and authorization. Read more at https://docs.altinn.studio/en/dialogporten/user-guides/authenticating/

All GET operations* and POST operations may return or contain, respectively, personal identifiable information (national identity numbers and names).

For more information about this product, see https://docs.altinn.studio/en/dialogporten

* Except the metadata APIs
info.title: Dialogporten EndUser

get.description: Resolves dialog metadata and authorization evidence for a supported instance reference.

400.description: Validation error occurred. See problem details for a list of errors.

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

403.description: Authenticated end user is not authorized for the supplied instance reference.

404.description: No dialog match was found for the supplied instance reference.

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

get.summary: Looks up a dialog by instance reference

get.description:
Performs a search for dialogs, returning a paginated list of dialogs. 

* All date parameters must contain explicit time zone. Example: 2023-10-27T10:00:00Z or 2023-10-27T10:00:00+01:00
* See "continuationToken" in the response for how to get the next page of results.
* hasNextPage will be set to true if there are more items to get.

parameters.description: Filter by one or more service owner codes
parameters.name: org

parameters.description: Filter by one or more service resources
parameters.name: serviceResource

parameters.description: Filter by one or more owning parties
parameters.name: party

parameters.description: Filter by one or more extended statuses
parameters.name: extendedStatus

parameters.description: Filter by external reference
parameters.name: externalReference

parameters.description: Filter by status
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Only return dialogs created after this date
parameters.name: createdAfter

parameters.description: Only return dialogs created before this date
parameters.name: createdBefore

parameters.description: Only return dialogs updated after this date
parameters.name: updatedAfter

parameters.description: Only return dialogs updated before this date
parameters.name: updatedBefore

parameters.description: Only return dialogs with content updated after this date
parameters.name: contentUpdatedAfter

parameters.description: Only return dialogs with content updated before this date
parameters.name: contentUpdatedBefore

parameters.description:
Only return dialogs that have content that has/hasn't been seen.
If null, no filtering is applied
If true, returns dialogs that have been seen
If false, returns dialogs that have not been seen
            
A dialog's content is considered seen if:
- It has been visited by the GET .../dialogs/{dialogId} endpoint since the last content update, and
- It does not have a system label MarkedAsUnopened.
parameters.name: isContentSeen

parameters.description: Only return dialogs with due date after this date
parameters.name: dueAfter

parameters.description: Only return dialogs with due date before this date
parameters.name: dueBefore

parameters.description: Filter by process
parameters.name: process

parameters.description: Filter by Display state
parameters.name: systemLabel

parameters.description: Whether to exclude API-only dialogs from the results. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: excludeApiOnly

parameters.description: Search string for free text search. Will attempt to fuzzily match in all free text fields in the aggregate
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Limit free text search to texts with this language code, e.g. 'nb', 'en'. Culture codes will be normalized to neutral language codes (ISO 639). Default: search all culture codes
parameters.name: searchLanguageCode

parameters.description: Supply "continuationToken" for the response to get the next page of results, if hasNextPage is true
parameters.name: continuationToken

parameters.description: Limit the number of results per page (1-1000, default: 100)
parameters.name: limit

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

403.description: Unauthorized to get the supplied dialog (not owned by authenticated organization or has additional scope requirements defined in policy).

404.description: The given dialog ID was not found.

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

get.description: Gets the list of activities belonging to a dialog

200.description: Successfully returned the dialog activity list.

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

403.description: Unauthorized to get the supplied dialog (not owned by authenticated organization or has additional scope requirements defined in policy).

404.description: The given dialog ID was not found.

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

get.summary: Gets a list of dialog activities

get.description: Gets a single activity belonging to a dialog.

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

403.description: Unauthorized to get child entity for the given dialog (dialog not owned by authenticated organization or has additional scope requirements defined in service identifiers policy).

404.description: The specified dialog ID or activity ID was not found.

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

get.description: Gets the list of label assignment logs belonging to a dialog

200.description: Successfully returned the dialog label assignment log list.

401.description: Missing or invalid authentication token. Requires a Maskinporten-token with the scope "digdir:dialogporten".

403.description: Unauthorized to get the supplied dialog (not owned by authenticated organization or has additional scope requirements defined in policy).

404.description: The given dialog ID was not found.

410.description: Entity with the given key(s) is removed.

503.description: Service Unavailable, used when Dialogporten is in maintenance mode

Retry-After.description: Delay before retrying the request. Datetime format RFC1123

get.summary: Gets a list of dialog label assignment logs

put.description:
Sets the system labels of the dialog.

Optimistic concurrency control is implemented using the If-Match header. Supply EnduserContextRevision to ensure that the context is not modified/deleted by another request in the meantime.

204.description: The dialog system label was updated successfully.

409.description: Conflict occurred while processing the request.

412.description: The supplied If-Match header did not match the current Revision value for the dialog. The request was not applied.
